title: Early Medieval art (quiz)
topic_path: Test your knowledge of Early Medieval art

assessment_items.question_content:
**The eagle was a popular barbarian motif during the Middle Ages, which represented . . . **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The eagle was a popular barbarian motif during the Middle Ages, representing the sun god to pagans and St. John to Christians.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/fibulae)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The illustrations of the *Lindisfarne Gospels* show us that Hiberno-Saxon artists had . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56ceb9ccfb913b84be3fa1afc63c5b4a.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The illustrations of the Lindisfarne Gospels show us that Hiberno-Saxon artists had a sophisticated sense of design and an intense interest in detail.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-lindisfarne-gospels)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Look at the Cross-Carpet page from the *Lindisfarne Gospels* and identify the typical features of Hiberno-Saxon art.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56ceb9ccfb913b84be3fa1afc63c5b4a.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The Cross-Carpet page shows knots, spirals, interlacing, and animal hybrids, which are typical of Hiberno-Saxon Art. It does *not* show a realistic representation of plants and animals or careful modeling of human figures.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-lindisfarne-gospels)

choices.content: realistic representations of plants and animals

assessment_items.question_content:
**Many of the objects in the Sutton Hoo Ship burial are associated with . . ..**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Many of the objects in the burial are associated with warfare, such as helmets and shields.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-sutton-hoo-ship-burial)

A fibula is a brooch with a body, pin, and catch that is commonly found in barbarian grave sites.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/fibulae)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What do the objects from the Sutton Hoo Ship Burial tell us about England c. 700 C.E.?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The objects from the ship burial reflect a time of political unrest and Christianization in England.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-sutton-hoo-ship-burial)

choices.content: it was a time of political unrest
it was a time of political stability
Christianity had not yet come to England

assessment_items.question_content:
**The *Lindisfarne Gospel* book was made at Lindisfarne Priory  in the early 8th century, but later that century, the book was saved by the monks from potential destruction by . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The Lindisfarne Gospels was saved by the monks at Lindisfarne Priory when Vikings from the north attacked and pillaged the monastery. 

[Watch this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/the-lindisfarne-gospels)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Grave goods provide the most concrete cultural information about barbarians, who . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Grave goods provide the most concrete cultural information about barbarians, who were mostly illiterate and did not leave behind written records.

[Read this to review](/humanities/medieval-world/latin-western-europe/early-medieval/a/fibulae)

choices.content: did not leave behind written records
were too busy pillaging to record their history
wrote their history in a language we have not yet translated
